topic: Copilot CLI hook/injection surface parity investigation

finding: Copilot CLI 1.0.34 exposes --effort/--reasoning-effort (low|medium|high|xhigh) as a CLI flag, --agent, --acp (Agent Client Protocol server), --additional-mcp-config, --allow-tool/--deny-tool, and interactive /agent, /skills, /mcp slash commands. This suggests a real extensibility surface with plugin/agent/skill/MCP registration, increasing the prior that outcome A (full parity) is achievable vs outcome C (no parity).

noveltyJustification: This pass added first-party evidence that Copilot CLI exposes sessionStart/userPromptSubmitted hooks, while showing that the documented hook and agent models still stop short of main-session prompt injection.

focus: hook mutation and custom agent loading
noveltyJustification: This pass converted earlier partial hook/agent evidence into a sharper answer: CLI hooks exist, but their outputs are ignored for prompt/session mutation, and custom agents remain delegated subagents rather than an always-on main-session injection surface.

focus: instructions skills mcp and acp boundaries
noveltyJustification: This pass tightened the remaining parity surfaces: custom instructions are the only documented native startup text channel, MCP stays pull-based, and ACP only becomes an injection path when Spec Kit acts as an external client wrapper.

focus: hooks output contract and custom agent boundaries
hooks output and agent selection boundaries
